data.title: Nu Skin targets placement of 100,000 Prysm iO devices in 2026 as company signals transformation and India expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Nu Skin Enterprises (NUS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Ryan Napierski, President and CEO, reported that "we delivered fourth quarter and 2025 results within our guidance range with a strong improvement in earnings, which resulted in a 40% increase in stock price for the year as we continue to focus on improving shareholder value amidst our strategic transformation." He highlighted the successful transaction of Mavely and the company's renewed focus on becoming "the world's leading intelligent beauty, wellness and lifestyle leadership opportunity platform."
  • Napierski emphasized the launch of Prysm iO, describing it as "a noninvasive carotenoid measurement device that provides intelligent insights into nutritional health across 4 critical domains of diet, fitness, lifestyle and nutritional supplementation." He reported the company has already collected "nearly 400 million intelligent wellness data points from 21 million scans of more than 10 million people in 50 countries."
  • Expansion into India was a key focus, with premarket operations initiated in November and a formal market opening anticipated in late 2026. Napierski stated that India "represents one of our most significant long-term geographic growth opportunities."
  • Looking ahead, "we are aiming to place more than 100,000 Prysm iO devices by the end of this year since the introduction in late '25," Napierski said. The company also aspires to bring Prysm iO to "10 million healthy households by 2030."
  • Napierski summarized, "In 2026, it is all about accelerating our evolution towards our intelligent beauty and wellness platform vision as we launch Prysm iO around the globe and expand our emerging market footprint by continuing growth in Latin America and expanding into India."
  • CFO James Thomas stated, "On an adjusted basis, we delivered $1.27 in earnings per share, up from $0.84 last year, representing about 51% growth." Thomas noted, "For the fourth quarter, we delivered revenue inside our guidance range at $370 million, with approximately $1 million headwind from foreign currency. Earnings per share was $0.29, in line with expectations, which closed out our annual performance near the high end of our original guidance range."

Outlook

  • For 2026, Nu Skin projects revenue in the range of $1.35 billion to $1.5 billion, including an estimated foreign exchange headwind of $13 million to $15 million or approximately 1%.
  • Earnings per share is anticipated between $0.80 and $1.20, with an expected tax rate of 35%. First quarter 2026 revenue is projected between $320 million and $340 million, and reported earnings per share in the range of $0.10 to $0.20.
  • Thomas explained, "we are modeling -- as per our guide in the release, we are 1% growth on the high end of our guidance and down 9% on the low end of guidance as we look due to the weighting of -- or the timing of the launch of the new product introductions towards the back half of the year."

Financial Results

  • Full year 2025 revenue was $1.49 billion, with a foreign currency headwind of approximately $13.4 million. Gross margin for Q4 was 70.7%, compared to 71.4% in the prior year. Within the core business, gross margin was 77.6%, up 100 basis points from the prior year.
  • Selling expense for Q4 was 35.5%. General and administrative expenses remain well managed, and operating margin for the quarter was 6.3%. Adjusted operating margin for the year was 6.7%, up 140 basis points from the prior year.
  • Nu Skin ended the quarter with approximately $240 million in cash and reduced outstanding debt to $224 million. Cash flow from operations for the year was $80.3 million. The company returned $11.8 million in dividends and $20 million in share repurchases during the year.

Q&A

  • David Storms, Stonegate Capital Partners, Inc.: Asked for more detail on Prysm iO's revenue contribution. Napierski responded that "the key to Prysm as we envision it, is the placement of these devices that will lead to subscriptions from customers over time," but specified the company is "not really yet at the point of saying exactly what the revenue conversion will be." He said device sales could reach "like $30 million in devices" based on "100,000 units," but subscription monetization is still being learned.
  • Storms asked about India. Napierski noted, "local manufacturing is one of our top priorities there because the -- obviously, import duties are so high, and we want to ensure high quality, great products at the right price there." Thomas added, "we're being a little bit cautious in how much we're actually forecasting in India. So we've tailored that back, but we believe in the high long-term potential of that market."
  • Storms requested thoughts on guidance leverage. Thomas explained, "we're working to bring our costs in line with revenue. So as we go throughout the quarters in 2026, we'll continue to work on our overhead, and we're continuing to refine that to get that into levels that are in line with our top line."
  • Storms asked about other portfolio storylines. Napierski highlighted "the restaging and rollout of Tru Face, which is our premium skin care line," and noted continued strong performance in Latin America, as well as improvements in China, Europe, and Southeast Asia.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed excitement around Prysm iO but pressed for more detailed revenue guidance and clarity on India’s impact, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone, while also probing for more specifics on key business drivers.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing achievements and strategic priorities. During Q&A, both Napierski and Thomas provided detailed responses but avoided giving specific forward projections for Prysm iO subscriptions and India’s revenue impact, indicating caution and a measured approach.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained confident about long-term prospects but was more explicit about the timing and scaling challenges in 2026, while analysts’ tone maintained interest but continued to seek more granular financial detail.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter placed greater emphasis on the launch and placement goals for Prysm iO, targeting 100,000 devices in 2026, compared to the previous quarter’s focus on groundwork and limited previews.
  • Guidance for 2026 was introduced with explicit revenue and EPS ranges, while the prior quarter’s guidance was more focused on narrowing ranges for 2025.
  • India’s entry was further along in Q4, with premarket operations underway, versus initial premarket planning in Q3. Both quarters highlighted Latin America’s strong performance, but the current quarter provided more detail on cash flow, debt reduction, and share repurchases.
  • Management’s confidence in the transformation was maintained, though slightly more cautious on near-term revenue contributions from new initiatives, and analysts continued to focus on the main growth drivers and profitability levers.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified inherent switching costs associated with the business transformation and realignment, particularly affecting 2025 and early 2026.
  • The formal India launch is anticipated in late 2026, with management highlighting challenges such as local infrastructure and the need for localized manufacturing to address high import duties.
  • The company noted ongoing top line pressures in specific markets and a higher expected tax rate for 2026, which could impact earnings per share.

Final Takeaway

Nu Skin’s leadership presented 2026 as a transformative year, with the global launch of Prysm iO and the strategic entry into India positioned as key growth drivers. Management emphasized disciplined financial execution, operational improvements, and a measured approach to scaling new initiatives. While mid- to long-term potential was highlighted, near-term guidance reflected caution on the timing and magnitude of new revenue streams, with a clear focus on delivering value through innovation, efficiency, and global expansion.
